hero.title: Ecologist focused on ecosystems, biodiversity and restoration.
hero.tagline:
I work at the intersection of field ecology, data analysis, and conservation practice—translating observations
          from the landscape into evidence-based decisions.

card.title: Ecology at a glance
card.text:
Adapt this box to highlight your core ecology profile: habitats you know best, taxa you work with, and how you
          like to contribute to research or conservation teams.

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: A short overview of who you are as an ecologist, your main interests, and the types of ecosystems you know best.
paragraphs:
Write 2–3 concise paragraphs here. For example: your academic background in ecology or related fields, any
            professional roles you’ve held, and the main ecological questions or conservation challenges that motivate you.

Mention specific ecosystems (e.g. temperate woodlands, freshwater systems, grasslands, urban greenspace), and
            the taxa you’re most experienced with (e.g. birds, plants, invertebrates, mammals, amphibians).

You can also briefly note your strengths: e.g. designing surveys, careful field data collection, GIS analysis,
            statistical modelling in R/Python, stakeholder engagement, or communicating findings to non‑scientists.

skillGroups.title: Field skills
items: Standardised survey methods (e.g. point counts, transects)

skillGroups.title: Data & analysis
items: R / Python (adapt to what you use)

experience.title: Experience
experience.subtitle: Roles, placements, and volunteer work that built your ecology expertise.

items.title: Role title · Organisation
items.description:
One or two sentences on what you did: habitats surveyed, survey methods, responsibilities, and any notable
            outputs (e.g. reports, datasets, recommendations).

items.title: Role title · Organisation
items.description:
Use more list items like this for other ecology jobs, internships, or long‑term volunteer positions. Emphasise
            concrete tasks and skills over generic duties.

projects.title: Selected projects
projects.subtitle: 3–6 concise case studies that show how you work and what you’ve contributed.

items.title: Project name – short description
items.description:
One paragraph describing the project goal, your role, the methods used, and the main ecological insight
            or outcome. If there is a public report or GitHub repo, link it here.

items.title: Another ecology project
items.description:
Use consistent structure for each case study so visitors can quickly scan what you did and what you learned
            from each piece of work.

publications.title: Publications & outputs
publications.subtitle: Papers, reports, posters, or talks related to your ecology work.

items.title: Author(s) (Year). Title of article/report.
items.description: One‑sentence summary of the ecological question and key finding. Include a link if the document is public.

items.title: Talk or poster title
items.description: Briefly describe who the audience was and what you presented.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Make it easy for people to reach you about ecology roles, collaborations, or further information.
paragraphs:
Replace this text with 2–3 sentences about the kind of opportunities you’re interested in (field seasons,
            research assistant roles, MSc/PhD opportunities, consultancy work, collaborations, etc.).

You can also mention when you’re available (e.g. upcoming field seasons) and whether you’re open to remote
            analysis work, report writing, or occasional travel.

rows.label: Download CV
rows.text: Add a link to your CV PDF once uploaded.
